perturbation.original_text: After the Closing Date (as defined in the Share Subscription Agreement), Tsinghua Unigroup and ChipMOS shall cooperate, expand, strengthen and stabilize the relationship with the related upstream, midstream, and downstream industries engaged in the assembly and testing services of LCD drivers, microelectromechanical systems (MEMS), the Internet of Things (IoT) and Radio Frequency Integrated Circuits (RFIC) and/or wafer bumping services in Mainland China.
perturbation.changed_text: After the Closing Date, Tsinghua Unigroup and ChipMOS shall cooperate to expand their relationship.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the phrase '(as defined in the Share Subscription Agreement),' the modified text creates uncertainty about which closing date is being referenced, as the Share Subscription Agreement is no longer explicitly linked. This is further compounded by removing the specific industry sectors for cooperation, broadening the scope to any expansion of their 'relationship'. If later clauses reference specific industry cooperation expected 'after the closing date as defined,' this omission will create an in-text contradiction, because there is no specific closing date being referenced.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Content of Strategic Alliance and Expected Benefits After the Closing Date, Tsinghua Unigroup and ChipMOS shall cooperate to expand their relationship. Tsinghua Unigroup shall also introduce other potential suppliers, customers and business partners in Mainland China to ChipMOS.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies cooperation related to specific industries ('LCD drivers, microelectromechanical systems (MEMS)... wafer bumping services'). The changed text removes this specificity, broadening the scope of cooperation. The contradicted text then repeats the broad scope of cooperation in a summary of the strategic alliance. While they match each other, it now contradicts the original intent of the agreement, as the summary no longer reflects the initially agreed-upon specific industries that the original agreement specifies that Tsinghua Unigroup and ChipMOS must cooperate in. Therefore, the omission of the specific industries in the Changed Text causes an in-text contradiction because it creates ambiguity about which closing date is being referenced, and removes reference to certain industries which the Contradicted Text expects. This aligns with the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed by, and construed in accordance with the laws of Taiwan. The Parties shall first seek to solve any dispute arising out of or related to this Agreement through negotiation. If the Parties fail to solve such dispute through negotiation, each Party shall have the right to issue notice ("Dispute Notice") to the other Party, and such Dispute Notice shall include the content of the dispute. If the Parties fail to resolve such dispute amicably through negotiation within sixty (60) days from the date on which a Party issues its Dispute Notice to the other Party, each Party shall have the right to submit such dispute to the Hong Kong International Arbitration Center, and proceed with the arbitration procedures in accordance with the Rules of the International Chamber of Commerce with three (3) arbitrators. Each Party shall each select one (1) arbitrator, and the third arbitrator shall be appointed by the two (2) arbitrators so selected. All language used in such proceedings shall be Mandarin Chinese. The Parties agree to keep the content of the dispute and the proceeding of the arbitration confidential. The arbitration award shall be final and binding on the Parties. The losing Party in such arbitration shall bear all of the costs and expenses related to the arbitration as determined by the arbitrators in such dispute (including attorney's fees).
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by, and construed in accordance with the laws of Taiwan. The Parties shall first seek to solve any dispute arising out of or related to this Agreement through negotiation. If the Parties fail to solve such dispute through negotiation, each Party shall have the right to submit such dispute to a mutually agreed-upon arbitration process. The arbitration award shall be final and binding on the Parties.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific details of the dispute resolution process, including the 'Dispute Notice,' the location of arbitration (Hong Kong International Arbitration Center), the rules (International Chamber of Commerce), the number of arbitrators, the language, and cost allocation, this change creates an in-text contradiction. The contract still requires arbitration, but provides no concrete method, leading to uncertainty. If other clauses depend on the specific detailed process that was just removed, that dependancy creates an internal contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Governing Law and Jurisdiction This Agreement shall be governed by, and construed in accordance with the laws of Taiwan. The Parties shall first seek to solve any dispute arising out of or related to this Agreement through negotiation. If the Parties fail to solve such dispute through negotiation, each Party shall have the right to submit such dispute to a mutually agreed-upon arbitration process. The arbitration award shall be final and binding on the Parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes key details of the dispute resolution process, including the specific arbitration venue, rules, number of arbitrators, language, and cost allocation. This contradicts the initial intent of the original text by creating ambiguity where there was once specificity. The contradicted text repeats the new changes, however the changes themselves cause a contradiction with the original contract. This reliance on a 'mutually agreed-upon arbitration process' without any guidelines or procedures makes it unclear how to proceed with arbitration, thus creating an in-text contradiction. The removal of previously defined terms causes the ambiguity.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties agree that the Parties will not disclose information in connection with the execution, existence, content, and performance of this Agreement to any third party before the Parties have made an announcement to the public pursuant to Section 3.11 of this Agreement. However, the foregoing restriction shall not apply to disclosure made to the board of the directors, management team, and relevant employees who need to know such information, attorneys, accountants, financial counsel, and competent authorities for the purposes of performing this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties agree to keep this agreement confidential.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the detailed exception clause that allowed disclosure to specific parties like directors, attorneys, and accountants, the modified text creates a much stricter confidentiality requirement. This conflicts with the original intent, especially as Section 3.11, dealing with public announcements, is still referenced in other sections. If Section 3.11 allows a specific action conditional on being shared by an auditor or accountant, but this new 3.9 states that they cannot be shared with anyone, this will create an internal contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Announcement The Parties shall not make an announcement to the public without the consent of the Parties regarding the execution and content of this Agreement and information in connection with the performance of this Agreement, which includes, but is not limited to the disclosure of material information, pursuant to the laws and the content thereof. The Parties shall negotiate and determine whether to make the announcement by press release, press conference or any other method and the content of the announcement. However, in the event that a Party discloses the above-mentioned information pursuant to the laws or requests made in judicial proceedings, and the disclosing Party could not obtain the consent of the other Party in time or the other Party refused to provide its consent without proper reasons after the disclosing Party notified the other Party of such situation, then the disclosing Party may disclose the above-mentioned information.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows for disclosure to specific parties, including legal counsel and accountants, while the changed text imposes a strict confidentiality obligation with no exceptions. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which outlines the process for making public announcements, potentially requiring disclosure to external parties like auditors or legal counsel, making those actions now impossible or a breach. The changed text now introduces a blanket confidentiality restriction, conflicting with the provisions in other parts of the agreement that might require or anticipate certain disclosures, thus preventing performance of the Announcement section.
